title: Crash-Candle Reversion v1 - OOS Result (2024-2025, run once under committed D12 protocol)
head_ref_note: HEAD 8b14308 (Crypto-D12 OOS protocol) at run; factory tree clean; nothing staged; runner deleted before pytest; no staging, no commit.

1_protocol_reference.rules_used: exact frozen CCR1 v1 - long-only spot; signal ret_1d <= -5%; entry open_{t+1}; exit close of 3rd bar (entry_index+2); no stop, no target, no trend filter, no confirmation filter; one position per symbol; ignore overlapping signals; run ONCE per symbol; no variants

2_is_baseline_reference.is_top3_removal_net_base: POSITIVE on BTC (+15.76%) AND combined (+314.55%) - the gate that fails OOS
2_is_baseline_reference.is_failed_gate: single-year dominance (BTC 2020 = 96% of net, combined 2021 = 98%); every symbol lost the 2022 bear

3_inputs.no_is_rows: 2020-2023 NOT included in OOS slice (rows_before_2024_used = 0 for all symbols)

4_data_qa.verdict: CLEAN - no defect; no IS contamination; no 2026 contamination

7_gate_evaluation.precedence: FAIL checked first and is absolute; ETH/SOL strength may NOT rescue a BTC-primary or combined FAIL

rule_mismatch.value: exact frozen CCR1 v1; all exits time_exit

fail_triggers_fired: top-3 removal makes combined net <= 0 (combined net ex-top3 @0.30% = -13.60%)
7_gate_evaluation.result: FAIL - two independent FAIL triggers fired

8_verdict.central_reason: The top-3-winner-removal gate (the universal killer) PASSED on BTC and combined IN-SAMPLE but FAILS OUT-OF-SAMPLE on both: BTC net ex-top3 = -12.21%, combined net ex-top3 = -13.60%. The OOS net is carried entirely by a handful of top winners; remove three trades and both BTC-primary and combined go net-negative. ETH is also net-negative OOS (-33.33% base) both years. SOL alone is robust (top-3 pass, both years positive) but ETH/SOL strength cannot rescue a BTC-primary / combined FAIL.
8_verdict.contrast_with_is: IS (D11) top-3 removal: BTC +15.76%, combined +314.55% (PASS). OOS (D13) top-3 removal: BTC -12.21%, combined -13.60% (FAIL). The single concentration weakness flagged at IS (regime/winner dependence) materialized OOS as winner concentration.

9_recommendation.action: PARK Crash-Candle Reversion v1 (CCR1) as OOS_FAIL
9_recommendation.no_rerun: No OOS rerun, no second look, no cherry-picked sub-window.
9_recommendation.no_tuning: Do NOT tune -5%, do NOT switch to -7%/-10%, do NOT add an SMA/trend or confirmation filter, do NOT change the 3-bar exit to chase the OOS - that overfits to the OOS. Any such change is a NEW pre-registered spec id and a fresh ladder, never an edit here.
9_recommendation.ladder_status: CCR1 v1 is the second parked crypto branch after CODR-1 (IS_FAIL). The crash-candle family is OOS-falsified at v1.
9_recommendation.scope: OOS 2026 stays SEALED. Perps remain BLOCKED. Crypto stays a SEPARATE research lane, never mixed with futures validation claims. S30 stays PARKED; futures branches untouched. No paper, no live, no exchange API, no broker authorized anywhere.

final_line: Crypto-D13 OOS ran ONCE under the committed D12 protocol and returned FAIL on the top-3-winner-removal gate (BTC and combined). CCR1 v1 is PARKED; no rerun, no tuning. OOS 2026 stays sealed; no crypto strategy is validated, paper-ready, or live-ready.
